title: Reading and Use of English - Part 2
description: Complete the text with ONE word only.

content.text: Rewilding is an innovative approach to conservation that lets nature take the (0) seat. Unlike traditional methods, (9) often involve active human management, rewilding aims to restore natural processes and let ecosystems repair themselves. In many cases, this involves the reintroduction of apex predators or 'keystone' species. || (10) it not for the return of wolves to Yellowstone Park, the entire river ecosystem might have remained degraded. Not (11) did the wolves control the deer population, but they also inadvertently changed the behavior of other animals, allowing forests to recover. (12) comes as no surprise that such projects are gaining popularity worldwide. However, (13) the clear ecological benefits, rewilding remains controversial among local farming communities. (14) far as these critics are concerned, the risk to livestock is simply too high. Nevertheless, (15) long as there is a dialogue between scientists and locals, rewilding could become a cornerstone of future environmental policy. It is a strategy (16) which many believe our planet's biodiversity depends.

questions.explanation: Relative pronoun used for a non-defining relative clause referring to 'methods'.
Inversion in a second conditional structure (Were it not for...).
Part of the negative inversion 'Not only... but also'.
Dummy subject for the fixed expression 'It comes as no surprise'.
Preposition of contrast followed by a noun phrase.
Part of the fixed phrase 'As far as... is concerned'.
Part of the conjunction 'So long as', meaning 'provided that'.
Preposition required by the verb 'depend' (depend on/upon which).
